In 1967 it shut & a single lock now works. [Pre 1887 was a flight of 5 locks] The boats were powered by sail or animals. When people (including the lock-keeper's family) pulled them they

pages.full_text:
were called porters— [?pickets] of dandelion-collector because of leaning so far forward. The repair workshop, which used independent clutches off a hydraulic turbine, used the [d] chevron gearing invented by Citroën [?]. It changed to electricity in 1943

   We managed to catch the ferry which left 40 min early (about [?9].10). There was quite heavy traffic on the drive home. We stopped at Spice Avenue for a balti at 8pm & heard about the Moseley Tornado from the waiter. All was well when we got home, but there were signs of heavy rain (washed gravel) in the garden.

      Saturday October 8th 9.45.
      Thomas & John joked at us for leaving 15 min late! Thomas is about to go to Russia John is working in the accounting office of Dudley Council.
   We reached __Duxford__ & looked at aircraft before & after lunch. At
pages.notes: Text continues from previous page (describing the Fontinettes boat lift) at the top. The phrase after 'porters—' is uncertain; could read 'pickets' or another word. The time 'about [?9].10' has a superscript character before '.10' that is not entirely clear — likely '9.10' (i.e. 9:10am). 'Moseley Tornado' refers to a local weather event (tornado in Moseley, Birmingham area). 'Saturday October 8th' falls on a Saturday in 2005, 2011, or 2016 within the volume's range (2003–2019); year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. Text cuts off at the bottom with 'We reached Duxford & looked at aircraft before & after lunch. At' — continues on next page. No handwritten page number visible.
